summary: Follow-up discovered while splitting policy safety invariants from data-driven registry and enum validation.

observations.problem: Policy loading now permits additive promotion decision and log status registry values while keeping the core PROMOTE/HOLD/DISCARD and pending/recorded/not_required values required. Downstream promotion report schemas and reporting/status consumers now have registry helper coverage for the current core decisions, but additive policy values remain descriptive until the executable gate contract is intentionally expanded.
observations.why_it_matters: A future policy evolution that intends to make an additive decision value executable must still coordinate schema, reducer, telemetry, trend aggregation, and apply-policy semantics rather than only a policy edit.
observations.automation_candidate: Automated on 2026-04-20 KST by adding promotion decision registry semantic helpers, wiring downstream outcome/status mapping to them, and pinning promotion report, trend, and telemetry schema enums to the registry contract in tests.
observations.suggested_followup: Keep additive policy registry values descriptive. If a new executable decision is introduced later, first add explicit registry semantics for outcome, terminal/finalizable behavior, ledger event type, and apply eligibility before expanding schemas and gates.
